overview: I want to serve in the Air Force with a path toward special operations. The Air Force Special Warfare (AFSWC) community has four enlisted paths: Combat Controller (CCT), Pararescue (PJ), Special Reconnaissance (SR), and Tactical Air Control Party (TACP). All are AFSOC / SOCOM-aligned and all embed with other SOF units across branches.
pipeline_update_2026: The Air Force Special Warfare Training Wing launched Pipeline Optimization in May 2026. A new 16-week 'Zulu Course' on combat shooting, movement, and small-unit communication is now Phase 2 for all AFSWC candidates, inserted after the 4-week Assessment and Selection Course. The redesigned A&S is now Phase 1.

physical_screening_test.run_1_5mi: Pass — competitive times vary by path

entry_requirements_all_paths.vision: Normal color vision and depth perception
entry_requirements_all_paths.additional: Qualify for marine diving duty AND parachute duty (medical standards)
entry_requirements_all_paths.personality_assessment: TAPAS (Tailored Adaptive Personality Assessment System) score — minimum varies by path (30 for CCT/TACP/SR, 60 for PJ)

shared_pipeline_phases.label: Basic Military Training (BMT)
shared_pipeline_phases.description: All Air Force enlisted members begin with 7.5 weeks of BMT. I learn Air Force structure, core values, and physical conditioning foundations.

shared_pipeline_phases.label: Special Warfare Candidate Course (SW Prep)
shared_pipeline_phases.description: 7-week intense preparation: running, rucking, swimming, strength and conditioning. I learn the history of Air Force Special Warfare and take the Initial Fitness Test (IFT) to determine if I advance to pipeline training.

shared_pipeline_phases.label: Special Warfare Assessment and Selection Course (Phase 1 — 2026 redesign)
shared_pipeline_phases.description: Four weeks of maximum-intensity assessment: obstacle courses, pool exercises, rucking, and constant evaluation of mental toughness and leadership potential. This is the gate. High attrition.

shared_pipeline_phases.label: Zulu Course — NEW 2026 Tactical Field Course (Phase 2)
shared_pipeline_phases.description: NEW as of November 2025. All AFSWC candidates attend this 16-week tactical field course before branching to specialty-specific training. Covers: shooting (marksmanship fundamentals and advanced CQB), moving under fire, small-unit communication, land navigation, and basic combat skills. Designed so every candidate arrives at specialty school with solid warrior foundation.
shared_pipeline_phases.launched: November 2025 per Air Force Special Warfare Training Wing Pipeline Optimization initiative

CCT.label: Combat Controller (CCT) — AFSC 1Z2X1
CCT.description: CCTs are the Air Force's air-to-ground integration specialists. They insert with ground SOF forces, provide terminal attack control for airstrikes, establish and control airfields in austere environments, and call in precision air support. All CCTs are FAA-certified air traffic controllers AND combat operators. CCTs deploy exclusively to AFSOC Special Tactics Squadrons (STS) and embed with SEALs, SF, and Rangers.
CCT.unique_skill: FAA-certified Air Traffic Controller qualification on top of full SOF combat skill set

post_zulu_pipeline.note: Longest segment — advanced weapons, demolitions, ATV ops, 5-level upgrade

CCT.assignment: Special Tactics Squadrons (AFSOC) — embedded with SEAL Teams, SF Groups, Ranger Battalions
CCT.career_ceiling: Senior CCTs become Special Tactics Team Sergeants or Commission as Special Tactics Officers (STOs)
CCT.tier1_path: CCTs can support and eventually be screened for classified Tier 1 support assignments (CAG, DEVGRU) as tactical air control attachments

PJ.label: Pararescue (PJ) — AFSC 1T2X1
PJ.description: PJs are the Air Force's combat search and rescue operators and personnel recovery specialists. Known as 'Guardian Angels,' they combine paramedic-level medical training with full SOF combat skills. PJs can be assigned to either ACC Rescue Squadrons (conventional) or AFSOC Special Tactics Squadrons (SOF). PJs treat and evacuate downed pilots and isolated personnel from behind enemy lines.
PJ.unique_skill: Nationally Registered Paramedic (NRP) qualification — the most medically trained of all AFSWC operators

post_zulu_pipeline.note: Produces Nationally Registered EMT, Nationally Registered Paramedic (NRP), combat medical skills, TCCC-CP, prolonged care, K9 TCCC, CBRN

assignment_options: AFSOC Special Tactics Squadron (SOF assignment — embeds with SEAL Teams, SF, Rangers)
ACC Rescue Squadron (combat rescue operations — more flying unit, less ground SOF)
PJ.career_ceiling: Senior PJs become Special Tactics Team Sergeants or transition to Combat Rescue Officers

SR.label: Special Reconnaissance (SR) — AFSC 1Z4X1
SR.description: SR is the newest AFSWC AFSC, redesignated from Special Operations Weather Team (SOWT) in 2019. SR operators provide environmental and pattern-of-life reconnaissance, clandestine intelligence collection, and battle space awareness. They operate deep behind enemy lines, often alone or in small teams, providing strategic reconnaissance for senior commanders.
SR.unique_skill: Advanced intelligence collection and environmental reconnaissance — works across all domains including denied access areas
SR.note: SR pipeline closely mirrors CCT through most common courses. SR-specific recon and intel collection training occurs at Keesler AFB, MS (8 weeks) and Pope AAF.
SR.assignment: Special Tactics Squadrons and joint SOF assignments

TACP.label: Tactical Air Control Party (TACP) — AFSC 1Z3X1
TACP.description: TACPs are experts in coordinating ground weapons systems and precision airstrikes. They embed with Army and Marine Corps ground units as Joint Terminal Attack Controllers (JTACs). TACP specialists in AFSOC Special Tactics Squadrons receive additional training including military freefall and attach to SOF units from all branches as precision strike experts.
TACP.unique_skill: JTAC qualification — the most widely deployable of AFSWC operators; works across all ground units from conventional to Tier 1

post_zulu_pipeline.note: Radio comms, small unit tactics, close air support basics

post_zulu_pipeline.stage: Initial Certification Course Phase 1 — Combat Field Skills
Initial Certification Course Phase 2 — Strike Phase

TACP.afsoc_upgrade_path: TACPs already serving can apply to AFSOC Special Tactics to receive freefall training and embed with SOF units. Must already be a qualified TACP within USAF.
TACP.assignment: Army Divisions (conventional) or AFSOC Special Tactics Squadrons (SOF)

roles: Special Tactics Team Sergeant (most senior enlisted role in STS)

career_progression.warrant_officer_path: Limited in USAF; senior CCT/PJ/SR can commission as Special Tactics Officers (STOs) through OCS
bbillet_options: Joint SOF Exchange (with Army SF, SEAL Teams, MARSOC)

pay_2026.note: 2026 basic pay — same DoD-wide pay table. 3.8% increase from 2025.

af_reclass.label: Air Force Reclass — Did Not Complete AFSWC Pipeline
af_reclass.description: If I do not complete Assessment and Selection or fail the IFT, I reclass to a different Air Force AFSC.

options.description: Air Force military police / base defense. Closest to combat arms in conventional AF.
options.note: Path to deploy with SOF or join AFSFC (Security Forces Center) tactical units

options.description: Embed with Army as JTAC — less intense pipeline than AFSOC track
Intelligence and signals; works with SOF support
If I have air traffic controller skills from partial CCT training
170+ AFSCs available across maintenance, logistics, medical, cyber, space, and more

college.TA: $4,500/yr — use during garrison, base assignments between deployments
college.CCAF_degree: Community College of the Air Force provides associate degrees aligned with AFSC — automatic enrollment, free

housing.first_assignment: Dorms (single) or BAH off-base / on-base family housing (married)

financial_rules: Pipeline is 18-30 months — delay major purchases until assigned to operational unit
CCAF degree is free — start Day 1
TSP from Day 1 — BRS government match requires at least 5% contribution
VA Loan when ready to buy — no down payment, no PMI
Combat zone tax exclusion applies in designated zones
